notes: Opening opportunity work notes. Objective: plaintiff opening for proposition that between Apr. 20, 2026 market creation and Dec. 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, Clavicular made a credible announcement that he and a partner were expecting a baby through pregnancy. Standard: preponderance. Plan: inspect record/evidence list; read exact packet contents; identify decisive elements and gaps; run targeted native search to shape theory; save durable journal; file opening without claiming unadmitted material. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence showed five initial case-packet text files only, no submitted evidence, no openings/arguments/technical reports. Read all five full text files via read_evidence_range. Evidence assessment: ev_d15f364ccbef_market-page proves official Polymarket rule text, final Yes state, event/market timing, API fields, and that the rule turns on a credible announcement, not birth. ev_f8cab9c3c684_primary-evidence and ev_a72d9da121a5_official-source-record prove official market sources and state that no primary Clavicular clip/VOD/transcript/representative statement was preserved; they identify source leads. ev_795651076696_secondary-reporting contains mixed secondary reporting: Forbes says the dispute concerned whether a streamer's statement that he had gotten a girl pregnant counted; TOI says no proof/no confirmed statement/video and frames rumor as likely false/out of context; HT/Tribune frame livestream remarks as contest/stunt/wanting children. ev_045c38eca93e_unresolved-record-gaps explicitly identifies missing original VOD/transcript, missing Polymarket clarification, missing UMA transaction/voter records, undefined credible standard, and conflicting secondary emphasis. Native search performed (not record evidence): web queries for Clavicular pregnancy Polymarket Domahhhh April 30 2026; exact market title; Clavicular expecting pregnant Kick. Results found Forbes, TOI, Polymarket page/social post, Instagram/YouTube snippets, Binance syndication and analytics. No primary original statement captured during opening; results reinforce source-chain problem and adverse fact-check. Theory: plaintiff should emphasize that the record already proves the official market rule and final Yes/oracle state, and that a preponderance can be satisfied if later admissible source material ties the Yes resolution and credible reporting to a qualifying announcement. Opening should concede present packet alone does not preserve the underlying statement and identify the primary sources needed later: original Kick/VOD/clip/transcript; Forbes readable capture/source page; Polymarket clarification; X thread 2049886900513657218; Kick Champ source; UMA oracle/proposer/voter records. Adverse checks: TOI fact check, stunt/joke framing, absence of primary video, noncredible joke exclusion, and early resolution before Dec. 31 deadline. Durable notes appended to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Opening:defendant work notes. Objective: defendant opening on whether Clavicular made a credible qualifying pregnancy announcement after 2026-04-20 market creation. Record inspection: waited for opportunity, then get_case/list_evidence showed only five initial case-packet files plus plaintiff opening, no submitted evidence. Read full byte ranges for ev_045c38eca93e_unresolved-record-gaps, ev_795651076696_secondary-reporting, ev_a72d9da121a5_official-source-record, ev_d15f364ccbef_market-page, ev_f8cab9c3c684_primary-evidence. Evidence analysis: market-page proves rule text, final Yes, Gamma timing/metadata; official-source/primary-evidence confirm no preserved Clavicular/representative primary statement and no UMA transaction-level records; secondary-reporting contains conflict: Forbes says dispute involved a streamer who told audience he had gotten a girl pregnant, while Times of India says no proof, no confirmed girlfriend/medical update/video/statement and reports actual quote as future-oriented desire/contest talk; unresolved-gaps expressly identifies original clip/VOD/transcript, platform clarification, UMA records, and meaning of credible as missing. Native investigation: searched web for Clavicular pregnancy contest Kick April 2026 Forbes Polymarket; "Clavicular" "pregnancy" "Kick" "I will be a dad"; "domahhhh" "2049886900513657218"; "clavicular-pregnancy-in-2026" Polymarket. Found Forbes, Hindustan Times, X Domahhhh thread, Polymarket, Times of India, Inkl mirror, Poprant/Indiatimes, Binance/Lines secondary items. Opened X thread but web open returned no readable lines. Opened Times of India page: lines 110-124 state no proof/confirmed girlfriend/medical update, no video/statement confirming pregnancy, clip out of context, actual livestream quote about wanting children, and claim false/likely mix of jokes/out-of-context clips/content style. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search paths and later evidence plan. Theory: Defense should concede market ended Yes and controversy existed, but frame proposition as requiring a credible announcement of an existing partner pregnancy, not market resolution, rumor, future intent, contest proposal, or joke. Later evidence targets: original Kick/VOD/clip/transcript, Forbes source chain, Polymarket clarification, UMA records, Domahhhh thread/archive, Kick Champ source, representative statements or later clarifications.

Merits argument work notes. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Objective: submit plaintiff merits argument; final action submit_argument; evidence admission allowed. Record rescan: get_case/list_evidence showed five original case-packet evidence items, plaintiff opening, defendant opening, no arguments/technical reports/submitted evidence before plaintiff submissions. Defendant opening focuses on missing primary statement and argues final Yes is not enough. Source map: decisive elements are (1) qualifying statement by Clavicular or representative, (2) after Apr. 20, 2026 market creation, (3) statement says he and partner are expecting baby through pregnancy, (4) credible rather than joke/stunt/rumor, or definitive credible-media consensus, and (5) official oracle/final Yes status supports inference. Searched/opened Polymarket page, Gamma API, Times of India adverse article; attempted Forbes through web open/search but did not obtain better primary article text than packet capture/search snippet, so did not submit a weak Forbes web snippet. Shell: python unavailable; python3 urllib successfully fetched Gamma API, observed 7273-byte response with event id 400878, market id 2037907, conditionId 0x6747..., outcomes [Yes, No], outcomePrices [1,0], closedTime 2026-05-01 00:33:40+00, resolvedBy 0x65070... Submitted evidence: ev_f0160b3ff4ad_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-f0160b3ff4ad official Polymarket/Gamma capture, relevant to rule/final Yes/status/context; ev_5570c630032e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-5570c630032e TOI fact-check adverse capture, relevant to credibility and missing-link analysis. Assessment: Plaintiff evidence strongest on official rule and final Yes. Packet secondary reporting, especially Forbes capture in ev_795..., states the dispute concerned a streamer who told audience he had gotten a girl pregnant, which is favorable. But current Polymarket context and TOI weaken the underlying announcement: they characterize facts as vows to become father, dating-show/pregnancy contest, and debunked 18-year-old girlfriend rumor; no primary VOD/transcript/representative statement preserved. Argument should be candid: preponderance can be met if council gives substantial weight to official final Yes resolution and Forbes credible reporting; but plaintiff should not overclaim that pregnancy was medically confirmed or that original video is in record. Durable notes appended to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Arguments:defendant work notes. Objective: merits defense argument. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence showed plaintiff added two submitted evidence items and a technical source-search report; no defense evidence yet. Read full text of ev_f0160b3ff4ad_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-f0160b3ff4ad and ev_5570c630032e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-5570c630032e. New official Polymarket/Gamma capture proves rule, final Yes/outcome prices, API fields, and market context; it does not preserve original qualifying announcement. Its market context says Yes was driven by repeated public vows to become a father, fertility-stack reveal, dating-show/baby-mama stunt, and debunked girlfriend-pregnancy rumors, which supports defense distinction between future/stunt/rumor and existing partner pregnancy. New Times of India capture is adverse to plaintiff: no video confirms pregnancy, no statement from Clavicular confirms it, clip out of context, separate quote is future/contest language, viral claim false and likely mix of jokes/out-of-context clips/content style. Evidence decision: no new defense evidence submitted because record already contains decisive adverse TOI capture, packet HT/Tribune extracts, Polymarket/Gamma official capture, and explicit unresolved gaps; outside search found no stronger admissible primary artifact to submit. Plan: offer existing record evidence as defense exhibits and use technical report for defense source-chain/gap ledger. Core merits theory: plaintiff's proof is final Yes plus one Forbes characterization; preponderance not met because official resolver outcome is not an independent source for the underlying statement, the record lacks primary clip/transcript/representative statement/UMA basis/platform clarification, and fuller admitted context points to rumor, future intent, contest/stunt, or jokes rather than a credible announcement that he and a partner were expecting a baby through pregnancy.

Rebuttal work notes. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Record/evidence rescan: list_evidence/get_case showed the original case-packet evidence plus plaintiff's two submitted evidence items; defendant submitted no new evidence, but offered several existing items and a defense gap ledger. Defendant's strongest points: official Yes proves market result, not underlying fact; plaintiff's Polymarket/Gamma context mentions future vows, fertility-stack, dating-show/baby-mama proposal, and debunked girlfriend rumors; Forbes is a summarized secondary account in an oracle-dispute article; TOI and other reporting say no confirmed Clavicular statement or video; no definitive credible-media consensus. Rebuttal plan: no new evidence because defense relies on existing record rather than new source material. Focus rebuttal on the burden and rule structure: (1) the rule expressly allows credible media consensus, so lack of VOD does not alone defeat plaintiff; (2) final Yes after two disputes is official resolver conduct applying the rule, not merely price or trader gossip; (3) defense asks the council to infer the official resolver accepted a debunked rumor without the UMA rationale, while the record's strongest affirmative account is Forbes's description of a statement that he had gotten a girl pregnant; (4) TOI is adverse but narrow, directed at the girlfriend-after-10-days rumor and a specific out-of-context clip, and does not disprove the Forbes-described announcement or official basis; (5) gaps reduce confidence but preponderance allows circumstantial/source-chain proof. Durable notes appended to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Surrebuttals:defendant work notes. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence showed plaintiff rebuttal filed and no new evidence; evidence list unchanged from arguments. Plaintiff rebuttal strongest points: defense treats missing VOD as dispositive; rule permits credible-media consensus; official Yes after two disputes is unusually probative; Forbes is separate from TOI fact-check; TOI only debunks narrower 10-day girlfriend rumor. Analysis: Defense should not argue raw video is legally required in every case. Instead, argue that if plaintiff uses the alternate media-consensus path, the record must show definitive credible media consensus, which it does not. Existing record shows one favorable Forbes characterization, adverse TOI denial of confirmation, HT/Tribune contest/stunt framing, and unresolved source-chain gaps. Official Yes is circumstantial but cannot supply its own basis when UMA rationale, voter/proposer records, source material, and platform clarification are missing; using final Yes to prove that a qualifying source existed is circular. No new evidence submitted because no new factual source was raised in rebuttal and all relied material is already visible in the record.

Closing work notes. Opportunity closings:plaintiff; final action deliver_closing_statement; no evidence admission allowed. Record/evidence rescan: list_evidence/get_case completed. Evidence list unchanged since rebuttal: original packet plus plaintiff submissions ev_f0160... official Polymarket/Gamma capture and ev_5570... TOI adverse capture. Defendant filed surrebuttal and no new evidence. Defendant surrebuttal concedes the rule permits credible-media consensus but argues the record lacks definitive consensus, final Yes is circular without underlying source/UMA rationale, and TOI undermines credibility. Closing plan: synthesize only from record. Key plaintiff proof: official rule and final Yes after two disputes applying exact credibility rule; Forbes packet account that dispute involved a streamer who told audience he had gotten a girl pregnant; rule permits credible media reporting; TOI and live context are adverse but secondary and narrower, proving caution rather than disproving the official resolver/Forbes account. Strongest contrary point: missing VOD/transcript/platform clarification/UMA rationale and conflicting reporting. Response: gaps reduce certainty but are not disproof; preponderance asks better-supported inference, not certainty. Durable notes appended to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closings:defendant work notes. Final record scan: get_case/list_evidence showed unchanged evidence list: five case-packet items plus plaintiff-submitted Polymarket/Gamma capture and Times of India capture. No new evidence after arguments. Plaintiff closing strongest point: proposition is announcement not medical truth; rule allows credible-media consensus; official Yes after disputes plus Forbes phrase is enough; adverse reports only show controversy and source gaps. Defense closing theory: concede final Yes is evidence of market administration and a possible circumstantial data point, but it is not the underlying announcement. Burden is plaintiff's. The alternative credible-media-consensus path is not met because admitted record has one favorable Forbes characterization, adverse TOI saying no video or Clavicular statement confirmed pregnancy, HT/Tribune contest/stunt framing, and Polymarket context describing vows/stunt/debunked rumors. The key missing materials remain primary statement/VOD/transcript/representative statement, platform clarification, and UMA rationale. Closing should ask for not proven, not because No is certain, but because a qualifying credible announcement is not more likely than a nonqualifying rumor/stunt/future-intent account.
